reviews.review_msg: Amazing recorded lectures that gave you everything you needed to know, fair assignments and nicely distributed across the semester, pretty hard exams but you are given a cheat sheet and if you do the assignments you will do great. Very kind professor too.

DO NOT take stats 201 online with this professor. His recorded lectures were incredibly difficult to understand. On top of that the lectures slides posted never worked properly, most of the material was always cut out from the slides so I used ai/the textbook to teach myself the entire course.

Do not take it with him, not helpful at all and tells different students different grading criterias and makes no sense. He will make jokes and laugh at the fact of getting a C-/failing. He says theres going to be no r coding and there is on the final thats worth 55%. Theres about 3-5 lecture recording every week and material coursework is heavy.
Final exam worth 55% and was very hard.

Gamage's STAT 203 class is engaging and well-structured. He explains complex concepts clearly with practical examples, making statistics fun. Lectures are engaging and assignments reinforce learning effectively. His supportive style fosters success, though the final exam is heavy, so consistent studying is really essential.

Best stats prof by far, in his lectures he breaks down concepts into very simple concepts for students to understand. Sometimes he will even add in if itll be tested on the exam. Although the final was worth 50% it's an online course so I guess it's understandable. Just wish he provided more practice content but overall highly recommend
Chill professor that makes what might be the driest subject matter even somewhat palatable. Plenty of time was given for the assignments and the tests allowed for the use of TYPED cheat sheets.
Best statistics professor!!!

I took this course in spring. Harsha is a very chill and approachable professor. His explanations are clear and his expectations are reasonable. Manageable workload. Fair exams, though the final was a bit tricky. Practice assignment and textbook questions well to prepare for exams.
best prof at sfu , made concepts really easy to understand. He replies to emails super quick, even at 12am. The tests are fair and mainly come out of the textbook

The best professor I could have wanted for STAT270. His lectures are clear and assignments are straightforward. Exams are also fair. He is super approachable and is always looking to help students. He is constantly seeking feedback to improve his course which is something I respect. He is very caring and wants to see his students do well. 10/10

Dr. Perera genuinely cares about students. He goes slowly during lectures and repeats important concepts. His office hours are quite helpful and he often mentions what type of questions will be on the exam during class. Still, it is a hard class so make sure to consistently practice outside of class. Take STAT 270 with him if you're able to!
switched from scott pai's section to this one and im so glad. prof perera can actually teach and responds extremely quickly to emails

He's really kind and is able to teach a really dense subject efficiently, he also teaches slowly in chapter three (probabilities) which is really nice of him, but 270 is pretty hard in general so make sure to practice the textbook questions he assigns. Overall he's great, you can tell he knows what he's doing :)
Nice

STATS 270 was not an option but Harsha is the best prof I've had so far. He's very respectful and kind and is always wanting feedback from his students. His exams are very fair and realistic as well. Would recommend 10/10 and to go to his office hours even if you don't have any questions.

I had held back on taking STAT203 for years and finally took it with Harsha. He made it feel easier and took his time explaining concepts. The assignments really helped prepare for tests and he even allowed cheat sheets which really helped. Would definitely recommend taking his class, especially for statistics!

Harsha is such a king, he's an amazing professor who made stat 203 a very doable and enjoyable class. He's super caring and will make sure everyone understands the concepts. I love how he would emphasize and repeat important concepts in the course. If you do the assignments and keep up with the lectures and course, you can do really well!

Everyone scared me and said STAT 201 is the hardest class in their degree, however Harsha made it one of the easiest! He would make you do all the hard calculations in the assignments that weren't worth much, and the exams which were worth more would have the most basic easiest questions ever. I studied two hours before the final and got full marks

I've had Harsha before and he was fine, but 430 was poorly done for an online class. Basically read the textbook word for word while screen-sharing it. Didn't breakdown the material or add anything helpful. Felt like I was listening to the textbook on Audible. I spent more time memorizing details than learning the reasons behind certain designs.
